mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions into equities by lowering discount rates and supporting risk appetite, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month when the easing path is persistent.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward returns are not positive in a majority of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL acceleration above target erodes real returns and raises the chance of tighter policy expectations, so S&P 500 forward returns should be negative over the next 20 trading days when inflation momentum turns up.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show negative average 20-day returns, or if positive SPX outcomes exceed 50% of triggered cases across a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a low-employment threshold signals weakening household income and slower earnings growth, which should depress equity prices over the following month as labor stress filters into demand and margins.
failure_condition: Reject if SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of months after UNRATE crosses above 4.8%, or if the signal's Rank IC is below 0.03 for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and HOUST both weaken together, the signal identifies broad final-demand softening and housing spillovers, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter because revenue growth expectations deteriorate.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous declines in RSAFS and HOUST do not lead to below-zero average 60-day returns, or if the interaction term has no incremental predictive power versus each series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A strengthening INDPRO trend indicates rising industrial output and capacity utilization, which should support earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when production momentum is sustained.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher than baseline following positive 3-month changes in INDPRO, or if the rolling 3-year signal IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An upside surprise in GDPC1 growth signals stronger aggregate demand and better profit growth, which should raise S&P 500 returns over the next quarter when real activity is expanding rather than slowing.
failure_condition: Reject if strong-GDPC1 observations do not produce positive average 60-day returns, or if negative returns remain dominant despite growth above 2.5% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A joint rise in PAYEMS and a stable or falling UNRATE indicates labor-market breadth without overheating, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month through stronger consumption and lower recession risk.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-UNRATE signal fails to improve 20-day return hit rates versus either series alone, or if positive returns are not more common when PAYEMS rises and UNRATE does not.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation remains elevated, the market should discount tighter policy persistence and lower equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation remains above 2.5% annualized
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate, high-inflation regimes do not generate negative 5-day forward returns, or if the joint condition is not more predictive than either proxy alone.
